Collaborate API Reference

Organizations worldwide face increasingly sophisticated and coordinated cybersecurity threats, making it crucial for security teams to adopt a collaborative approach to define their security strategy. Collaborate is a situational awareness platform that enables real-time threat alert sharing and aggregation, helping you effectively respond to threats through information sharing, coordination, and collaboration among security analysts. With Collaborate, you can enrich, anonymize, and share precise threat intelligence, including indicators of compromise (IOCs) and incident responses. Collaborate's API is organized around REST and is available to all customers. The API endpoints contain predictable resource-oriented URLs, return JSON-encoded responses, and use standard HTTP response codes and authentication.

For AI agents and LLM-based discovery, see the Collaborate LLM-friendly API Guide.

The following are the two portals in Collaborate:

Analyst Portal

The Analyst Portal is an administrative module mainly used by administrators and analysts to create and share information with members. Administrators set the application and user preferences for all users through the Analyst Portal.

Member Portal

The Member Portal is used by security teams and member organizations for receiving strategic intelligence and alerts. It works along with the Analyst Portal to enhance communication and strategic intelligence sharing among employees and member organizations.

Supported Collaborate Version: 3.8.6.0 and later versions

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Base URL

Production:

https://tenantname.cyware.com/api/

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Overview

The Analyst Portal is an admin module mainly used by administrators and analysts. Analysts create and share information with members. Administrators set the application and user preferences for all users through the Analyst Portal.

As an analyst, you can perform the following actions:

  • Alert Management: Create and publish alerts about vulnerabilities, exploits, and security issues to members.
  • Organization Management: Create and manage organizations, control their status, and access related data for efficient alert sharing.
  • Manage Intel Submissions: Manage threat intel submissions from members to enable bi-directional sharing of critical information as alerts across the organization.
  • Manage Request for Information (RFI): Manage RFIs submitted by members, review and publish them as alerts, and foster collaboration to enhance security.
  • Intelligence Requirements: Retrieve Intelligence Requirements (IRs) and their followers.
  • Allowed Indicators: Add trusted indicators to your allow list to enhance alert quality and support informed decision-making.
  • Manage Members: Add and manage members, modify their details and status, and access member information as needed.
  • Doc Library: Manage and share files, documents, and attachments to facilitate coordination between analysts and members.
  • Tag Library: Organize and manage tags to efficiently categorize and locate items within the platform.

For more information, see Collaborate Analyst Portal Documentation.

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Authentication

In Collaborate's Analyst Portal, you can manage user access and feature permissions by defining Open API roles and generating credentials to control access.

Create Open API Roles

You can create user roles for members to regulate access to Collaborate features using open API. You can assign these roles to members while generating API credentials for them. For more information, see Create Open API Roles.

Generate Open API Credentials

You can generate open API credentials for analysts or members to access the open API endpoints. You can assign a role to control and restrict members' access to Collaborate's features. For analysts, after the v3.7.6.0 update, access to certain categories is based on your user role in the Analyst Portal. This update does not impact credentials generated prior to this version. For more information, see Generate Open API Credentials.

Mandatory Query Parameters

To authenticate the API requests made to Collaborate, you must include the following parameters in the query of each API request:

1

Access ID

Indicates the access ID of your Collaborate open API credentials.

2

Expires

Indicates the validity of the signature parameter. The signature becomes invalid if not used within the specified expiry duration. You can use

expires = current time + 300 seconds for authentication. You can give a margin of 10 to 15 seconds for expiry.

3

Signature

A combination of the access ID, secret key, and expires parameters to authenticate API requests. The signature is hashed using the HMAC-SHA1 algorithm and then encoded using the Base64 scheme.

  • Create a StringToSign value using the StringToSign = access_id + \n + expires formula.
  • Create a Signature value using the Signature = Base64(HMAC-SHA1(secret_key, UTF-8-Encoding-Of(StringToSign))) formula.

Test API Request Format

For all PUT and POST calls, the content-type header is mandatory.

You can now make a test API call in the following format using the Access ID, Expires, and Signature values:

Plain Text
base_url/csap/v1/list_alerts/? Expires=expires_value&AccessID=accessid_value&Signature=signature_value

Remember to replace base_url, expires_value, accessid_value, and signature_value.

{"content-type": "application/json"} is to be sent as part of the headers unless specified otherwise.

A status code 415 response is given in the case of an invalid content type being provided.

Use the script in the Pre-request Script section in the right panel to generate the Expires and Signature parameters in Postman.

Endpoints

GET

v1/test_connectivity/

1var accessid = pm.environment.get("accessid"); 2var secret_key = pm.environment.get("secretkey"); 3/* 4 -Generating epoch time 5 -Epoch time is being sent in Expires parameter of the request 6 -Signature is generated using the accessid and expires parameter 7*/ 8pm.environment.set("unixtimestamp", Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)); 9var expires = pm.environment.get("unixtimestamp") + 300; 10pm.environment.set('UTC Timezone', new Date().toISOString()); 11pm.environment.set("expires", expires); 12var to_sign = accessid + "\n" + expires; 13/* 14Generate Signature for Authentication 15 -Key-based hashing is done using HMAC-SHA1 algorithm 16 -This is then converted into Base64 format 17 -Finally, the generated signature is URL-encoded 18*/ 19const CryptoJS = require('crypto-js'); 20var hash = CryptoJS.HmacSHA1(to_sign, secret_key); 21var hashInBase64 = CryptoJS.enc.Base64.stringify(hash); 22pm.environment.set("signature", encodeURIComponent(hashInBase64)); 23/* 24Set authentication credentials in request parameters 25*/ 26pm.request.addQueryParams(`AccessID=${accessid}`) 27pm.request.addQueryParams(`Signature=${pm.environment.get('signature')}`) 28pm.request.addQueryParams(`Expires=${pm.environment.get('expires')}`)

Alerts

Collaborate helps you share real-time situational awareness alerts on the latest incidents, breaches, malware, and vulnerabilities to members over email, web, and mobile platforms. For more information about Alerts, see Collaborate Alerts Documentation.

As an analyst, you can perform the following actions to keep members informed and prepared:

  • Create Alerts: Create alerts with essential details, including a title, summary, Traffic Light Protocol (TLP), category, and recipients.
  • Add Comprehensive Information: Add additional information to the alert such as images, campaigns, attachments, and more.
  • Incorporate Threat Indicators: Specify threat indicators that identify alert elements, enhancing clarity and specificity for members.
  • Enable Threat Response: Enable members to respond to organization-specific threats by attaching Threat Defender Library (TDL) content.
  • Recommend Actions: Recommend actionable steps based on the threat insights shared in the alert, enabling members to act effectively.
  • Attach Threat Assessments: Attach threat assessments for members impacted by a cyber threat.

As an analyst, you can create and update alerts and access all related data.

Endpoints

POST

csap/v1/create_card

POST

csap/v1/alert_update/

GET

csap/v1/list_alert/?status=status&start_time=1737405600&end_time=1737441600&page_size=10&p...

GET

GET

csap/v1/get_alert_history/{short_id}/

GET

csap/v1/list_category/?page=2&page_size=10&q=threat

GET

csap/v1/list_category/{category_id}/

GET

csap/v1/list_additional_fields/

GET

csap/v1/list_additional_fields/?field_id=f792fcc62d462

v1/tag/

Organization

Analysts can classify members according to organizations while inviting them to the Collaborate Member Portal. Organizations can also be further grouped into organization types and be associated with certain organizational levels. This allows analysts to share alerts quickly with members from selected organizations and organization types. For more information, see Collaborate Organization Documentation.

As an analyst, you can perform the following actions:

  • Create and Manage Organizations: Create organizations, assign them to members, and update organization details for seamless alert sharing.
  • Activate or Deactivate Organizations: Enable or disable organizations as needed to align with operational requirements.
  • Retrieve Organization Data: Access detailed lists of organizations, organization types, and organization levels in Collaborate.
  • Retrieve Associated Details: Obtain domains and IP addresses linked to a specific organization.

Endpoints

POST

csap/v1/create_organization/

PUT

csap/v1/organization_details_update/

PUT

csap/v1/organization_activation/

GET

v1/get_organization_list/

GET

csap/v1/get_organization_type/

GET

csap/v1/organization/{organization_id}/ip_domains/?type=type

GET

csap/v1/organization_levels/?page=1&page_size=10

Intel

A member can submit threat intel reports from the Member Portal. The intel submissions include information to help you, as an analyst, understand the risks of an organization's most common or severe external threats. Analysts can further share this information as alerts to all the other members of the organization. This facilitates the sharing and dissemination of information between the Analyst and the Member Portals. For more information, see Collaborate Intel Submission Documentation.

As an analyst, you can perform the following actions:

  • Retrieve and View Intel: Access a list of intel submissions and view detailed information for specific intel.
  • View Intel Category Details: Access intel categories and the list of fields and details associated with an intel category.

Endpoints

GET

v1/list_intel_reported/?page=page&page_size=page_size

GET

csap/v1/get_intel_detail/{incident_id}

GET

csap/v1/list_intel_category/

GET

csap/v1/reports/form_details/{category_id}/

Allowed Indicators

Add trusted indicators like IPs, domains, URLs, email addresses, and hashes to your allow list. These indicators are parsed as allowed indicators when creating alerts, improving the quality of information shared, and assisting members in making more informed decisions. For more information, see Collaborate Allowed Indicators Documentation.

Endpoints

GET

csap/v1/allowed_indicators/?page=page&page_size=page_size&type=type&value=value

POST

csap/v1/allowed_indicators/

Dashboard

Dashboards in Collaborate help you visualize data, providing an overview of key metrics and insights. These visualizations allow you to monitor activities, track trends, and make informed decisions based on the data available. For more information, see Collaborate Dashboard Documentation.

As an analyst, you can use the APIs in this section to perform the following actions:

  • Retrieve a List of Widgets: Access a list of widgets available on the Collaborate dashboard.
  • Get Data for a Widget: Fetch the data associated with a specific widget. This allows you to retrieve detailed information and analyze it further for deeper insights or reporting purposes.

Endpoints

GET

csap/v1/widget_library/?is_active=true&widget_origin=custom_defined&page=1&page_size=10&q=...

GET

csap/v1/widget_library/data/?widget_id=widget_id

Member

Members are users of the Collaborate Member Portal who receive alerts from security experts (Publishers, Creators) on the Member Portal (web or mobile application). With the features provided by the Member Portal, members can stay updated with crucial information and prepare for the potential impact of cyber events and other developments.

As an analyst, you can add Collaborate members as well as modify and manage members, including setting their status to inactive. For more information, see Collaborate Member Documentation.

Endpoints

POST

csap/v1/create_end_user/

PUT

csap/v1/update_member/

GET

csap/v1/member/?organization_id=organization_id&email=email&page=page&page_size=page_size

GET

csap/v1/member/{user_id}/

POST

csap/v1/locations/

POST

csap/v1/recipient-group/

POST

csap/v1/end-users-status/

Messenger

Collaborate Messenger allows members to collaborate with each other, create groups for discussion, and start discussions based on topics. For more information, see Collaborate Messenger Documentation.

As an analyst, you can perform the following actions:

  • Retrieve Messages in a Topic: Access messages within a specific topic in Collaborate Messenger.
  • Get Topics: Retrieve a list of topics related to a specific alert in a group, helping you to track the discussion.
  • Retrieve Message Attachments: Get the attachments associated with messages on a particular topic.

Endpoints

GET

csap/v1/get_topic_messages/?topic_id=grp7WDklY1e23s&from_time=160000000&to_time=1710147669...

GET

csap/v1/get_alert_topics/?alert_id=alert_id&group_id=group_id

GET

csap/v1/get_messages_attachments/?seq_id=seq_id&topic_id=topic_id&expiration_time=expirati...

Reports

Reports provide statistics on different aspects of Collaborate such as member status, details of alerts published, and reader details.

Doc Library

The Doc Library is a secure place to store text, image, and video files where analysts and members can store and maintain important cybersecurity files. For more information, see Collaborate Doc Library Documentation.

As an analyst, you can perform the following actions in this section:

  • Upload a File: Upload a file to the Doc Library and share it with recipient groups or individual recipients.
  • Access Document: Retrieve details of a document or folder from the Doc Library.
  • Create a Folder: Create a folder in the Doc Library within a parent folder or in the root directory.
  • Access Folder Details: Retrieve details of a folder or the root directory.

Endpoints

POST

csap/v1/upload_media/

GET

csap/v1/upload_media/?document_id=document_id

POST

csap/v1/folder-details/

GET

csap/v1/folder-details/?folder_id=0888cb50-f234-422b-8765-65bdc62fa819&page=page&page_size...

GET

csap/v1/folder-details/?page=page&page_size=page_size

Request for Information (RFI)

In Collaborate, members can submit Requests for Information (RFIs), facilitating the exchange of insights for optimizing their organization's security posture. As an analyst, you can review these RFIs and publish them as alerts to a larger network. For more information, see Collaborate Request for Information Documentation.

As an analyst, you can perform the following actions:

  • Retrieve a list of all RFIs, providing an overview of current and past requests.
  • Access detailed information about a specific RFI, including its context and related information.
  • View all responses received for a particular RFI, helping analysts assess the quality and relevance of the collected data.

Endpoints

GET

csap/v1/rfi/

GET

csap/v1/rfi/{incident_id}/

GET

csap/v1/rfi_responses/{incident_id}/

Intelligence Requirements (IRs)

Intelligence Requirements (IRs) are information requests that help you gain insights into a particular subject over a specified period. IRs guide the collection, analysis, and dissemination of intelligence to support decision-making and threat detection within your organization. For more information, see Collaborate Intelligence Requirements Documentation.

Endpoints

GET

csap/v1/irs/

GET

csap/v1/ir_followers/{ir_id}/

GET

csap/v1/ir-alerts/?ir_id=01JKKKN1HQZE9KCEC10VXTEJ3E&page=1&page_size=10

Recommended Actions

In an alert, actions are tasks assigned or recommended to members. This is based on the insights shared in the alert or as a course of normal security measures. As an analyst, you can recommend actions to members and retrieve the list of recommended actions.

For more information, see Collaborate Recommended Actions Documentation.

Endpoints

POST

csap/v1/recommended_action/

GET

csap/v1/recommended_action/?is_recommended_action=true&alert_id=5b8d53c7&page=1&page_size=...

Overview

Members use the Member Portal widely to collaborate, receive, and share security information. Member Portal is a web-based application that members can use from desktop or laptop computers.

As a member, you can perform the following actions:

  • Receive Alerts: Stay informed with real-time alerts on security incidents, breaches, vulnerabilities, and emerging threats.
  • Collaborate through Messenger: Engage in secure, real-time communication with other members, create group discussions, and share insights on specific topics.
  • Share Threat Intelligence: Submit valuable threat intelligence to help organizations understand external threats. All submissions are reviewed and verified by analysts before being shared.
  • Access Organization: Manage organizations, control their status, and access related data.
  • Request for Information (RFI): Gather information from analysts and other members to share relevant and contextual information on threats, vulnerabilities, malware, and prioritized intelligence requirements.

For more information, see Collaborate Member Portal Documentation.

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Authentication

Create Open API Roles

As an analyst user of Collaborate's Analyst Portal, you can create user roles for members to regulate access to Collaborate features using open API. You can assign these role to members while generating API credentials for them. For more information, see Create Open API Roles.

Generate Open API Credentials

Analysts of Collaborate's Analyst Portal can generate open API credentials for analysts or members to access the open API endpoints. They can also assign a role to control and restrict members' access to Collaborate's features. For more information, see Generate Open API Credentials.

Mandatory Query Parameters

To authenticate the API requests made to Collaborate, you must include the following parameters in the query of each API request:

  1. Access ID: Indicates the access ID of your Collaborate open API credentials.
  2. Expires: Indicates the validity of the signature parameter. The signature becomes invalid if not used within the specified expiry duration. You can use the expires = current time + 300 seconds for authentication. You can give a margin of 10 to 15 seconds for expiry.
  3. Signature: A combination of the access ID, secret key, and expires parameters to authenticate API requests. The signature is hashed using the HMAC-SHA1 algorithm and then encoded using the Base64 scheme.

Create a StringToSign value using the StringToSign = access_id + \n + expires formula.

Create a Signature value using the Signature = Base64(HMAC-SHA1(secret_key, UTF-8-Encoding-Of(StringToSign))) formula.

Test API Request Format

For all PUT and POST Calls being made, the content-type header is mandatory.

You can now make a test API call in the following format using the Access ID, Expires, and Signature values: base_url/csap/v1/list_alerts/? Expires=expires_value&AccessID=accessid_value&Signature=signature_value Remember to replace base_url, expires_value, accessid_value, and signature_value. {"content-type": "application/json"} is to be sent as part of the headers unless specified otherwise.

A status code 415 response is given in the case of an invalid content type provided.

Use the script in the Pre-request Script section in the right panel to generate the Expires and Signature parameters in Postman.

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Endpoints

GET

csap/v1/test_connectivity/

1import base64 2import hashlib 3import hmac 4import time 5import requests 6import urllib.parse 7# Credentials to access CSAP open API 8base_url = "<enter base URL>" # The base URL should contain / in the end. Example: https: //csap-01.cyware.com/api/ 9access_id = "<enter access ID>" 10secret_key = "<enter secret key>" 11expiration_margin_time = 20 # seconds 12# Just for formatting 13padding_length = 100 14padding_design_char = "-" 15tab_width = 25 16def signature(access_id: str, secret_key: str, expires: int) -> str: """ 17 Computes and returns signature. 18 """ 19 #string to sign in 20 to_sign = "{}\n{}".format(access_id, expires) 21 hashed = hmac.new( 22 secret_key.encode("utf-8"), to_sign.encode("utf-8"), hashlib.sha1 23 ) 24 # b64_signature 25 return base64.b64encode(hashed.digest()).decode("utf-8") 26def expiry_time(margin: int = expiration_margin_time) -> int: """ 27 Expiration time with the margin specified 28 """ 29 return int(time.time() + margin) 30def query(access_id: str, secret_key: str, expiration_margin_time: int, 31 method: str, endpoint: str, data = None, headers={}, **params): """ 32 Uses 'requests' module to get response. 33 Note: Payload for PUT and POST call can be specified in data (dict). 34 """ 35 expires = expiry_time(expiration_margin_time) # Getting expiry time 36 params[ 37 "Expires" 38] = expires 39 params[ 40 "AccessID" 41] = access_id 42 params[ 43 "Signature" 44] = signature(access_id, secret_key, expires) 45 url = "{}{}".format(base_url, endpoint) + "?" + urllib.parse.urlencode(params, doseq=True) 46 print("Using python-requests module".center(padding_length, padding_design_char)) 47 print("REQUEST DATA".center(padding_length, padding_design_char)) 48 request_data_dict = { 49 "Method": method, 50 "Params": params, 51 "Header": headers, 52 "Payload": data, 53 "URL": url 54} 55 for key, val in request_data_dict.items(): 56 print("{:<{width}}{}".format(key+":", val, width=tab_width)) 57 response = requests.request(method, url, headers=headers, json=data) 58 print("RESPONSE DATA".center(padding_length, padding_design_char)) 59 request_data_dict = { 60 "Status-code": response.status_code, 61 "Data": response.text 62} 63 for key, val in request_data_dict.items(): 64 print("{:<{width}}{}".format(key+":", val, width=tab_width)) 65 return response 66def test_connectivity(): """ 67 CSAP Open API test connectivity call. 68 """ 69 print("Test Connectivity".center(padding_length, padding_design_char)) 70 # Note the endpoint shouldn't begin with a '/'. base_url already contains it. 71 endpoint = "csap/v1/test_connectivity/" 72 method_type = "GET" 73 response = query(access_id, secret_key, expiration_margin_time, method_type, endpoint) 74if __name__ == "__main__": 75 test_connectivity()
Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Test Connectivity

Test connectivity with Collaborate's Member Portal.

Response

200
Object

Response Attributes

Successstring

Returns the status of the connection to Collaborate's Member Portal.

GET

/csap/v1/test_connectivity/

Python
1import requests 2import json 3 4url = "https://yourdomain.cyware.com/api/csap/v1/test_connectivity/" 5 6payload = {} 7headers = { 8} 9 10response = requests.request("GET", url, headers=headers, data=payload) 11 12print(response.text) 13

Response

Alerts

Collaborate helps you share real-time situational awareness alerts on the latest incidents, breaches, malware, and vulnerabilities to members over email, web, and mobile platforms. For more information, see Collaborate Alerts Documentation.

As a member, you can view and engage with alerts relevant to your organization’s security needs. You can perform the following actions:

  • Access Alerts: Receive timely alerts on vulnerabilities, exploits, and security concerns specific to certain products, industries, or organizations, keeping you informed of emerging threats and critical security issues.
  • Share Feedback: Provide feedback on alerts directly through the Member Portal, ensuring analysts receive valuable insights to refine and enhance their security updates.

As a member, you can access all alert-related data that has been shared with you.

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

GET

csap/v1/list_alert/?status=status&start_time=start_time&end_time=end_time&page_size=page_s...

GET

v1/get_news/?keyword=keyword

GET

csap/v1/list_category/?page=2&page_size=10&q=threat

GET

csap/v1/list_category/{category_id}/

GET

csap/v1/list_additional_fields/

GET

csap/v1/list_additional_fields/?field_id=f792fcc62d462

POST

csap/v1/tag/

GET

csap/v1/tag/

GET

csap/v1/list_info_source/

GET

csap/v1/channels/?channel_type=default_channel&pagesize=10&page=1

GET

csap/v1/get_location_list/?page=page&page_size=page_size&search=search

POST

csap/v1/alert_feedback/

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Get Alerts

Returns a list of alerts from Collaborate.

Changelog

Release v3.8.11 (EA)

Added support for the channel_ids query parameter.

Query Parameters

statusstring

Pass the status of the alerts to be retrieved. Published alerts are retrieved based on the published time in epoch format.

Default value
PUBLISHED
Enum values:
PUBLSIHED
start_timenumber

Pass the start date of the query in epoch format. This represents the earliest timestamp for any alert in the query.

end_timenumber

Pass the end date of the query in epoch format. This represents the latest timestamp for any alert in the query.

page_sizenumber

Pass the number of records in a page.

Default value
10
pagenumber

Pass the page number for the response.

Default value
1
category_idstring

Pass the category ID to filter alerts. Enter multiple category IDs separated by a comma. You can retrieve category_id using the Get Category List endpoint. Example, 4bfd32c0,c7012fcc

tlpstring

Pass the TLP to filter alerts. Enter multiple TLPs separated by a comma. Example, GREEN, WHITE.

titlestring

Pass the title to filter alerts.

channel_idsstring

Pass the channel IDs to filter alerts. You can enter multiple channel IDs separated by a comma.

Response

200
Object

Response Attributes

countnumber

Returns the total number of alerts in the Analyst Portal.

dataarray

Returns the ID, title, status, and published time of the alert.

Show child attributes

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

GET

/csap/v1/list_alert/?status=&start_time=&end_time=&page_size=&page=&category_id=&tlp=&title=&channel_ids=85396c98-e5c4-45a7-b831-9675f42b9e11

Python
1import requests 2import json 3 4url = "https://yourdomain.cyware.com/api/csap/v1/list_alert/?channel_ids=85396c98-e5c4-45a7-b831-9675f42b9e11" 5 6payload = {} 7headers = { 8} 9 10response = requests.request("GET", url, headers=headers, data=payload) 11 12print(response.text) 13

Response

{
  "count": 67,
  "data": [
    {
      "short_id": "cf946a91",
      "title": "Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise",
      "status": "PUBLISHED",
      "published_time": 1700848985
    }
  ]
}
Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Get Alert Details

Returns details of an alert using its unique ID. As a member, you can only retrieve published alerts that are shared with you.

Changelog

Release v3.8.9

Returns the source_urls response parameter.

Path Parameters

short_idstring Required

Pass the 8-digit unique ID of the alert for which you want to retrieve the details. You can retrieve the alert ID using the Get Alerts endpoint.

Response

200
Object

Response Attributes

short_idstring

Returns the 8-digit unique ID of the alert.

titlestring

Returns the title of the alert.

contentstring

Returns the description of the alert.

statusstring

Returns the status of the alert.

tlpstring

Returns the TLP assigned to the alert.

card_grouparray

Returns the list of recipients (both recipient groups and individual recipients) to whom the alert is published.

Show child attributes

individual_recipientsarray

Returns the list of individual recipient details to whom the alert is published.

Show child attributes

recipient_groupsarray

Returns the list of recipient groups to whom the alert is published.

Show child attributes

card_categoryobject

Returns the category assigned to the alert.

Show child attributes

card_infostring

Returns the additional details associated with the alert.

info_sourcearray

Returns the list of information sources for the alert.

Show child attributes

card_imagestring

Returns the image URL with the extension.

push_requiredboolean

Returns the push notification status of the alert.

push_email_notificationboolean

Returns the email notification status of the alert.

published_timenumber

Returns the epoch timestamp of when the alert was published. For draft alerts, the value is null.

optional_fieldsobject

Returns all the optional fields associated with the alert

indicatorsobject

Returns the indicator details attached to the alert.

Show child attributes

credibilityobject

Returns the credibility details associated with the alert.

confidenceobject

Returns the confidence level details of the alert.

announcement_typeobject

Returns the announcement type details of the alert.

riskobject

Returns the risk details associated with the alert.

urgencyobject

Returns the urgency details of the alert.

severityobject

Returns the severity details of the alert.

priorityobject

Returns the priority details of the alert.

kill_chain_phaseobject

Returns the kill chain phase details of the alert.

exploit_likelihoodobject

Returns the likelihood of an exploit for the alert.

exploited_wildobject

Returns the vulnerability details if already being exploited by attackers in the cyber landscape.

systemsaffectedobject

Returns the number of systems affected by the alert.

threat_actorarray

Returns the list of threat actors associated with the alert.

threat_methodarray

Returns the threat methods associated with the alert.

detectionmethodarray

Returns the detection methods associated with the alert.

root_causearray

Returns the list of root causes for the alert.

cyber_threat_methodarray

Returns the list of cyber threat methods associated with alert.

physical_threat_methodarray

Returns the list of physical threat methods associated with the alert.

document_typearray

Returns the list of document types associated with the alert.

vendorsnproductarray

Returns the list of vendors associated with the alert.

reportsourcearray

Returns the list of report sources for the alert.

vulnerability_typearray

Returns the list of types of vulnerabilities.

vulnerability_sourcearray

Returns the list of sources of vulnerabilities.

targeted_sectorarray

Returns the list of sectors that would be targeted.

card_tagarray

Returns the tags associated with cards.

Show child attributes

parent_alertsobject

Returns a list of parent alerts if the specified short_id corresponds to an updated alert.

child_alertsobject

Returns a list of child alerts if the specified short_id corresponds to an updated alert.

tactic_technique_pairs_dataarray

Returns the list of tactic techniques related to the alert.

custom_fieldsobject

Returns the custom fields associated with the category of the alert.

tracking_idstring

Returns the tracking ID associated with the alert. The tracking ID is used as reference for third-party integrations with Collaborate.

attachmentsarray

Returns any attachments (ID, file path, and file name) associated with the alert.

Show child attributes

source_urlsarray

Returns the names and URLs of the references associated with the alert.

Show child attributes

eventobject

Returns all the details of the event (location, URL, event type, schedules) if the category of the alert is Event.

intel_idobject

Returns the associated intel ID if it exists.

rfi_idobject

Returns the associated RFI ID if it exists.

shared_byobject

Returns an object containing details of the member who submitted the intel, including email, full name, and user ID.

Show child attributes

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

GET

/csap/v1/get_alert_detail/{short_id}

1import requests 2import json 3 4url = "https://yourdomain.cyware.com/api/csap/v1/get_alert_detail/685ca5d7" 5 6payload = {} 7headers = { 8} 9 10response = requests.request("GET", url, headers=headers, data=payload) 11 12print(response.text) 13

Export Alert

Returns an alert in downloadable format.

Save the response to your device, ensuring to add the .pdf extension to the file name so it downloads as a PDF.

Path Parameters

alert_idstring Required

Pass the 8-digit unique ID of the alert for which you want to export. Example: 473e4b78

Response

200
Object
Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

GET

/csap/v1/alert-pdf/{alert_id}/

Python
1import requests 2import json 3 4url = "https://yourdomain.cyware.com/api/csap/v1/alert-pdf/473e4b78/" 5 6payload = {} 7headers = { 8} 9 10response = requests.request("GET", url, headers=headers, data=payload) 11 12print(response.text) 13

Response

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Get Alerts by Keyword

Returns latest published alerts based on a specific keyword.

Query Parameters

keywordstring Required

Pass the keyword with alert title and summary. This is applicable for published alerts only.

Response

200
Object

Response Attributes

titlestring

Returns the title of the alert.

published_datestring

Returns the date when the alert was published.

card_categorystring

Returns the category of alert.

contentstring

Returns the description of alert.

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

GET

/v1/get_news/?keyword=

Python
1import requests 2import json 3 4url = "https://yourdomain.cyware.com/api/v1/get_news/?keyword=" 5 6payload = {} 7headers = { 8} 9 10response = requests.request("GET", url, headers=headers, data=payload) 11 12print(response.text) 13

Response

[
  {
    "title": "IC3 Issues Alert on Ransomware",
    "published_date": "2019-10-05",
    "card_category": "Alert",
    "content": "Released October 4, 2019 by IC3: Alert on ransomware threats"
  }
]
Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Get Alerts by Related Indicators

Returns published alerts by indicators mentioned in the alerts.

Body Parameters

indicator_typestring Required

Pass the indicator type to retrieve associated alerts.

indicator_valuestring Required

Pass the indicator value.

countsnumber

Pass the number of records you want to retrieve.

Default value
5

Response

200
Object

Response Attributes

countnumber

Returns the count of alerts.

alert_idarray

Returns the unique alert ID of the alerts which consist of the specified indicators.

Show child attributes

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

POST

/v1/get_related_alerts/

Python
1import requests 2import json 3 4url = "https://yourdomain.cyware.com/api/v1/get_related_alerts/" 5 6payload = json.dumps({ 7 "indicator_type": "MD5", 8 "indicator_value": "5622f67a4f071c421d37f40457a5e360", 9 "counts": 1 10}) 11headers = { 12} 13 14response = requests.request("POST", url, headers=headers, data=payload) 15 16print(response.text) 17

Response

{
  "count": 10,
  "alert_id": [
    "66865e0f"
  ]
}
Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Get Alerts by Tracking ID

Returns alerts using the tracking ID. A tracking ID is generated when third-party integrations publish alerts in Collaborate.

Query Parameters

tracking_idstring Required

Pass the tracking ID for the alert. You can retrieve the tracking ID using the Get Alert Details endpoint.

countsnumber

Pass the number of alerts you want to retrieve using the tracking ID.

Default value
5

Response

200
Object

Response Attributes

countinteger

Returns the number of alerts associated with the tracking ID.

alert_idarray

Returns the array of unique IDs of the alert.

Show child attributes

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

/v1/get_tracking_id_details/?tracking_id=237d56d5-b0e7-4ba1-a068-b7ac802b1c63&counts=

Response

{
  "count": 1,
  "alert_id": [
    "10031fdb"

Get Category List

Returns the list of active alert categories in Collaborate.

Query Parameters

pagenumber

Pass the page number to retrieve records. Default value is 1, which retrieves the latest records.

Default value
1
page_sizenumber

Pass the number of records to retrieve per page.

Default value
10
qstring

Pass a keyword to search for categories.

Response

200
Object

Response Attributes

countinteger

Returns the total number of categories.

resultsarray

Returns the category ID, name, and associated fields.

Show child attributes

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Get Category Details

Returns details of an alert category using the unique category ID. You can retrieve the category ID using the List Category endpoint.

Path Parameters

category_idstring Required

Pass the unique ID for the category. You can retrieve category_id using the Get Category List endpoint.

Response

200
Object

Response Attributes

countnumber

Returns the number of categories.

resultsarray

Returns the category ID, name, and associated fields.

Show child attributes

Get Additional Fields List

Returns the list of all fields in Collaborate.

Response

200
Object

Response Attributes

keystring

Returns the type of field. System denotes sytem fields and custom denotes custom fields.

display_namestring

Returns the display name of custom and system fields.

valuesarray

Returns field objects that define various attributes of a field.

Show child attributes

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Get Additional Field Details

Returns the details of a specific field in Collaborate using the field_id. You can retrieve field_id using the Get Additional Fields List endpoint.

Query Parameters

field_idstring Required

Pass the unique ID of the field. You can retrieve the field_id using the Get Additional Fields List endpoint.

Response

System Field
Object
The following are the response parameters of a system field:

Response Attributes

detailarray

Returns the details of the system field.

Show child attributes

Custom Field
Object
The following are the response parameters of a custom field:

Response Attributes

detailarray

Returns the details of the custom field.

Show child attributes

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Create Tag

Create a tag in Collaborate's Tag Library. Members can use tags while submitting threat intel RFIs and more.

Body Parameters

tag_namestring Required

Pass the name of the tag. Tag names must be within 100 characters.

Response

200
Object

Response Attributes

tag_namestring

Returns the name of the tag you created.

tag_idstring

Returns the unique ID of the tag.

is_activeboolean

Returns if the tag is in active status.

tag_slugstring

Returns the name slug associated with the tag.

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

POST

/csap/v1/tag/

Python
1import requests 2import json 3 4url = "https://yourdomain.cyware.com/api/csap/v1/tag/" 5 6payload = json.dumps({ 7 "tag_name": "Sample Tag" 8}) 9headers = { 10} 11 12response = requests.request("POST", url, headers=headers, data=payload) 13 14print(response.text) 15

Response

{

  "tag_id": "27b7b69e-2f43-4c1c-b969-3cc72463d3e3",
  "is_active": true,
  "tag_slug": "sample-tag"
}

Get Tags

Returns a list of all the tags in the Tag Library.

Response

200
Object

Response Attributes

tag_namestring

Returns the name of tag.

tag_idstring

Returns the unique ID of the tag.

is_activeboolean

Returns true if the tag is in active status.

tag_slugstring

Returns the slug associated with tag.

Get Recipient Groups

Returns a list of recipient groups that the member is part of.

Query Parameters

pagenumber

Pass the page number for the response. Default value is 1, which retrieves the latest records.

Default value
1
page_sizenumber

Pass the number of records in a page.

Default value
10
qstring

Pass a keyword to search for recipient groups. Example, manager.

all_databoolean

Pass true to retrieve details of all the recipient groups. Pass false to retrieve data based on the page and page_size.

Default value
false

Response

200
Object

Response Attributes

group_idstring

Returns the 8-digit unique ID of the recipient group.

group_namestring

Returns the name of recipient group.

group_tlpstring

Returns the TLP associated with recipient group.

group_typestring

Returns the recipient group type (system or custom).

allowed_for_intel_submissionboolean

Returns true if members of the recipient group have permission to share intel with other members of the recipient group.

allowed_for_rfi_submissionboolean

Returns true if members of the recipient group have permission to share RFI with other members of the recipient group.

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

GET

/csap/v1/list_recipient_group/?page=2&page_size=5&q=manager&all_data=

Get Channel List

Retrieves a list of channels based on the specified channel type.

Changelog

Release v3.8.11.0 (EA)

This endpoint is available starting from the 3.8.11.0 (Early Access) version.

Query Parameters

channel_typestring Required

Pass the channel type to retrieve channels.

Enum values:
default_channelmy_channelsubscribed_channel
pagesizenumber

Pass the number of records to retrieve on each page.

Default value
10
pagenumber

Pass the page number for the response. Default value is 1, which retrieves the latest records.

Default value
1

Response

My Channel
Object
The following are the response parameters when retrieving a list of channels with channel_type = my_channel

Response Attributes

countnumber

Returns the total number of channels returned in the response.

resultsarray

Returns an array of channel objects that match the query.

Show child attributes

Default or Subscribed Channel
Object
The following are the response parameters when retrieving a list of channels with channel_type = default_channel or subscribed_channel

Response Attributes

countnumber

Returns the total number of channels returned in the response.

resultsarray

Returns an array of channel objects that match the query.

Show child attributes

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Get Member Locations

Returns locations configured in the CSAP Analyst Portal.

Query Parameters

pagenumber

Pass the page number for the response. Default value is 1, which retrieves the latest records.

Default value
1
page_sizenumber

Pass the number of records in a page.

Default value
10
searchstring

Pass the location name of the member location you want to search for. You can search for multiple locations by separating each location with a space. Example, US India.

Response

200
Object

Response Attributes

countnumber

Returns the total number of locations.

dataarray

Returns the location details, such as location ID and name.

Show child attributes

What made this section unhelpful for you?

/csap/v1/get_location_list/?page=&page_size=&search=

1import requests 2import json 3 4url = "https://yourdomain.cyware.com/api/csap/v1/get_location_list/" 5 6payload = {} 7headers = { 8} 9 10response = requests.request("GET", url, headers=headers, data=payload) 11 12print(response.text) 13

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Submit Alert Feedback

Submit feedback for an alert by indicating like or dislike, rating the content and relevancy, as well as adding a comment.

For a successful submission:

  • alert_ID and like parameters are mandatory.
  • If you include relevancy_rating, you must also include content_rating, and vice versa.

Body Parameters

likenumber Required

Pass the value denoting your preference for the alert. Pass 0 for clearing previous feedback, 1 for like, and 2 for dislike.

Enum values:
012
alert_idstring Required

Pass the 8-digit unique alert ID for which you want to submit feedback. You can retrieve this using the Get Alerts endpoint.

commentstring

Pass the comment for the alert.

relevancy_ratinginteger

Pass your rating for the alert's relevancy using any value from 1 to 5, where 1 is the least relevant and 5 is the most relevant. If you pass this parameter, you must also pass content_rating in the request.

Min length
1
Max length
5
content_ratinginteger

Pass your rating for the alert's content using any value from 1 to 5. If you pass this parameter, you must also pass relevancy_rating in the request.

Min length
1
Max length
5

Response

200
Object

Response Attributes

detailstring

Returns the status indicating whether the feedback was submitted successfully.

card_updatedstring

Returns the 8-digit unique alert ID for which feedback is submitted.

Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

POST

/csap/v1/alert_feedback/

Python
1import requests 2import json 3 4url = "https://yourdomain.cyware.com/api/csap/v1/alert_feedback/" 5 6payload = json.dumps({ 7 "like": 1, 8 "alert_id": "9bd49110", 9 "comment": "This is informative!", 10 "relevancy_rating": 4, 11 "content_rating": 5 12}) 13headers = { 14} 15 16response = requests.request("POST", url, headers=headers, data=payload) 17 18print(response.text) 19

Response

{
  "detail": "success",
  "card_updated": "9bd49110"
}
Was this section helpful?

What made this section unhelpful for you?

Organization

Analysts can classify members according to organizations while inviting them to the Collaborate Member Portal. Organizations can also be further grouped into organization types and be associated with certain organizational levels. This allows analysts to share alerts quickly with members from selected organizations and organization types. For more information, see Collaborate Organization Documentation.

As a member, you can perform the following actions:

  • Retrieve Organization Data: View detailed lists of organizations, organization types, and organization levels you belong to.
  • Retrieve Associated Details: Access domains and IP addresses linked to your organization.
  • Update Organization Details (Member Admins Only): If you are a member admin, you can update the details of your organization.

Endpoints

PUT

csap/v1/organization_update/

GET

csap/v1/get_organization_list/

GET

csap/v1/get_organization_type/

GET

csap/v1/organization/{organization_id}/ip_domains/?type=type

Intel

Threat intelligence sharing helps organizations understand the risks of the most common and severe external threats. As a member, you can create and share threat intel information with analysts. Analysts review the shared threat intelligence and can choose to publish this information as alerts to other members. For more information, see Collaborate Threat Intel Documentation.

As a member, you can perform the following actions related to threat intelligence:

  • Submit Threat Intel: Submit threat intel to share information about external threats with analysts.
  • View Intel Submissions: Access a list of submitted intel and view detailed information for specific intel submissions.
  • Access Intel Categories: Retrieve information about available intel categories and their associated fields.

Endpoints

POST

csap/v1/report_intel/

GET

csap/v1/list_intel_reported/?page=page&page_size=page_size

GET

csap/v1/get_intel_detail/{incident_id}

GET

csap/v1/list_intel_category/

GET

csap/v1/reports/form_details/{category_id}/

Allowed Indicators

Add trusted indicators like IPs, domains, URLs, email addresses, and hashes to your allow list. These indicators are parsed as allowed indicators when creating alerts, improving the quality of information shared, and assisting members in making more informed decisions. For more information, see Collaborate Allowed Indicator documentation.

Endpoints

GET

csap/v1/allowed_indicators/?page=page&page_size=page_size&type=type&value=value

POST

csap/v1/allowed_indicators/

Messenger

Collaborate Messenger allows members to collaborate with each other, create groups for discussion, and start discussions based on topics. This section contains API endpoints that support messenger actions. For more information, see Collaborate Messenger Documentation.

As a member, you can perform the following actions:

  • Retrieve Messages in a Topic: Access messages within a specific topic in Collaborate Messenger.
  • Get Topics: Retrieve a list of topics related to a specific alert in a group, helping you to track the discussion.
  • Retrieve Message Attachments: Get the attachments associated with messages on a particular topic.

Endpoints

GET

csap/v1/get_topic_messages/?topic_id=grp7WDklY1e23s&from_time=160000000&to_time=1710147669...

GET

csap/v1/get_alert_topics/?alert_id=alert_id&group_id=group_id

GET

csap/v1/get_messages_attachments/?seq_id=seq_id&topic_id=topic_id&expiration_time=expirati...

Request for Information (RFI)

In Collaborate, members can submit Requests for Information (RFIs), facilitating the exchange of insights for optimizing their organization's security posture. For more information, see Collaborate Request for Information Documentation.

As a member, you can perform the following actions:

  • Submit a new RFI to request specific information from members or external sources.
  • Provide a response to an open RFI, contributing valuable insights or data.
  • Attach relevant files or documents to an RFI response for further clarity and support.

Endpoints

POST

csap/v1/rfi/

GET

csap/v1/rfi/

GET

csap/v1/rfi/{incident_id}/

GET

csap/v1/rfi_responses/{incident_id}/

POST

csap/v1/create/rfi_responses/{alert_id}/

POST

csap/v1/upload_attachment/rfi_responses/

Intelligence Requirements (IRs)

Intelligence Requirements (IRs) are information requests that help you gain insights into a particular subject over a specified period. IRs guide the collection, analysis, and dissemination of intelligence to support decision-making and threat detection within your organization. For more information, see Collaborate Intelligence Requirements Documentation.

As a member, you can perform the following actions:

  • Retrieve a list of IRs you submitted.
  • Retrieve a list of matched alerts for a specified IR ID.

Endpoints

GET

csap/v1/irs/

GET

csap/v1/ir-alerts/?ir_id=01JKKKN1HQZE9KCEC10VXTEJ3E&page=1&page_size=10

Recommended Actions

In an alert, actions are tasks assigned or recommended to members. This is based on the insights shared in the alert or as a course of normal security measures. As a member, you can suggest recommended actions to other members.

For more information, see Collaborate Recommended Actions Documentation.

Endpoints

POST

csap/v1/recommended_action/

GET

csap/v1/recommended_action/?is_recommended_action=True&alert_id=80287c5c&page=1&page_size=...
